text:
Once there was a wise old owl named Oliver.

One crisp autumn day, Oliver sat on a gnarled oak tree counting his acorn piles. The forest had been quiet since the birds had taken their leaves.

Suddenly, a small creature fluttered down onto the trunk beside Oliver. It was a squirrel, with pointed ears twitching nervously.

"I haven't seen you before," chuckled Oliver.

"I don't think a squirrel likes acorns?" the squirrel replied. "There's a predator lurking among the squirrels!"

The sly old owl laughed softly.

"How do you keep yourself so safe?" the squirrel asked curiously.

"Well," Oliver mused, "for one thing, I make only a handful of really good acorn meals during the year. I've learned not to trust every bird song."

"Oh," the squirrel said thoughtfully, clutching a furry pecan shell. "Do you have any brothers?"

Oliver's eyes twinkled with amusement.
